**ATTENTION: Important Notice**

Finish Reading

System Notice: Clear Your Browser Cache and Cookies for EDJOIN

Due to recent EDJOIN updates, your browser may still be using older website data stored in its cache or cookies. This can prevent the latest updates from loading correctly and may cause unexpected behavior when accessing EDJOIN.

If you experience any issues, please clear your browser cache and cookies using the instructions below.

Google Chrome

Open Chrome.

Click the three-dot menu (?) in the upper-right corner.

Select Delete browsing data.

Set the Time range to All time (or at least Last 7 days, if preferred).

Select the following:

Cookies and other site data

Cached images and files

Click Delete data.

Close and reopen Chrome.

Sign back in to EDJOIN.

Safari (macOS)

Open Safari.

From the top menu, select Safari > Settings (or Preferences on older macOS versions).

Go to the Privacy tab.

Click Manage Website Data.

Search for edjoin.org (or select Remove All if preferred).

Click Remove (or Remove All), then Done.

Next, select Safari > Settings > Advanced and enable Show Develop menu in menu bar (if it is not already enabled).

From the top menu, select Develop > Empty Caches.

Close and reopen Safari.

Sign back in to EDJOIN.

If you continue to experience issues after clearing your browser cache and cookies, please contact the Help Desk for further assistance.

# Verify Your Email Address

We recently updated the security of this site and all account emails must be verified to login.

# Email Verification Link Sent

An Email Verification link was sent to the email address . The verification link will expire in 48 hours.
Please click on the link in the email you received to continue and complete the verification process.


If you do not see the email in your inbox after approximately 10-15 minutes, **check your SPAM/Junk email folder(s)**, thank you.
